free_text: A study of 31,32,33P by the use of (a,p) reactions on
silicon isotopes.
Radial Ridge cyclotron.
A 25 MeV beam of alpha particles.
The self-supporting targets were manufactured by
resistive evaporation of SiO2 to a thickness of
approximately  100 mu-g/cm**-2. Natural silicon
dioxide was used for the reactions on 28Si and silicon
dioxide enriched with 29Si or 30Si was used for the
reaction on those isotopes. The target thicknesses from
the silicon component were caculated from elastic
scattering data at 12.5 degr.
Pairs of solid state detectors formed
DELTA E-E telescopes, four of which were placed
5 degr. apart on each of two arms in the scattering
chamber.
These detectors were used for particle
identifictaion in cojunction with an analogue
multiplier.
The zero-range DWBA calculations were performed.
